item.description:
# Clip 1.0.0
The first public release of Clip, a native macOS menu-bar recorder for quickly capturing and sharing clear screen recordings.
## Highlights
- Record a selected area, an app's visible windows, or an entire display, with optional microphone and system audio, cursor capture, pause, and resume. - Preview recordings immediately, trim the beginning and end, rename the file, and remove audio before sharing. - Export native-size H.264 MP4 files using Crisp, Compact, or Smallest quality; drag them into another app, copy them to the clipboard, or use Save As. - Reopen recordings from History and manage the Copy and drag exports linked to them, including revealing or deleting files. - Configure shortcuts, countdown, recording defaults, quality values, retention, launch at login, and other behavior in Settings.
## Updates
Version 1.0.0 installs Clip's in-app updater. This first updater-enabled version must be downloaded and installed manually from the DMG. Future releases can be found automatically or on demand with **Check for Updates…** in Clip.
## Requirements and first launch
Clip requires macOS 15 or later on Apple silicon.
This build is Apple Development signed but is not Developer ID signed or notarized. macOS may therefore block its first launch after download. If that happens, try opening Clip once, then go to **System Settings → Privacy & Security** and choose **Open Anyway**. Clip will also ask for Screen Recording permission before it can capture the screen; microphone and system-audio access are requested only when those options are enabled.
